item.title: Many Needles in a Haystack accepted at ICML 2026
item.description: Excited to share that our paper "Many Needles in a Haystack: Active Hit Discovery for Perturbation Experiments" has been accepted at ICML 2026 — see you in Seoul! 🇰🇷
item.content:encoded:
Excited to share that our paper "Many Needles in a Haystack: Active Hit Discovery for Perturbation Experiments" has been accepted at ICML 2026 — see you in Seoul! 🇰🇷

CRISPR and single-cell perturbation screens can test thousands of genes, but budgets are tight and the genes that actually move a phenotype are rare and scattered. The hard question is no longer can we screen? — it's which perturbations should we run next?

We tackle this with a lab-in-the-loop design loop: an AI model proposes a batch of genes to perturb, the lab runs them, the readouts update the model, and the cycle repeats — each round getting smarter about where the hits are hiding.

The catch is that standard active-learning methods chase a single "best" gene, but biology rarely works that way: hits sit in multiple pathways and cellular contexts. Our method, Probability-of-Hit, instead asks the right question — which genes are most likely to cross the hit threshold? — and recovers more biologically meaningful hits across five real immunology screens (T-cell activation, NK cytotoxicity, tau, SARS-CoV-2).

The upshot for experimentalists: more hits per plate, fewer wasted wells.

Congratulations to first authors Andrea Rubbi and Arpit Merchant, postdoc Sam Ogden, Amir Hossein Hosseini Akbarnejad, and thanks to Pietro Liò and Sattar Vakili for the great collaboration.

item.title: DirMoE at ICLR 2026 — a fully differentiable Mixture-of-Experts router
item.description: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) is a powerful way to scale large language models (LLMs): instead of running the full model for every token, a router activates only a few "experts," giving more capacity at roughly the same compute.
item.content:encoded:
Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) is a powerful way to scale large language models (LLMs): instead of running the full model for every token, a router activates only a few "experts," giving more capacity at roughly the same compute.

But routing is still a sore spot. Most MoE systems use Top-k + Softmax, where expert selection is discrete—so you don't get clean end-to-end gradients. In practice, this can lead to unstable routing, calibration issues, and uneven expert usage.

In our ICLR 2026 paper, we introduce DirMoE — a fully differentiable probabilistic router that separates which experts fire (Bernoulli) from how their weights are assigned (Dirichlet). We also add a simple "sparsity knob" (Simpson-index penalty) to control the expected number of active experts, without relying on load-balancing losses that can homogenize experts.

Results: DirMoE matches or exceeds vanilla MoE throughput (no extra bottlenecks), is strong/competitive on zero-shot benchmarks (ARC, BoolQ, PIQA, …), and leads to clearer expert specialization (interpretable domain focus like ArXiv/Books/GitHub code).

Led by Hesam Asadollahzadeh and Amirhossein Vahidi.

Read the paper on OpenReview · Thread on X

item.title: Our lab is awarded 2.9M$ from Medical Research Council to tackle kidney cancer using generative AI + massive multimodal data
item.link: https://lotfollahi.com/blog/Our lab is awarded 2.9M$ from Medical Research Council to tackle kidney cancer
item.guid: https://lotfollahi.com/blog/Our lab is awarded 2.9M$ from Medical Research Council to tackle kidney cancer
item.description: I am excited to announce that our lab has been awarded 2.9M$ from the Medical Research Council to tackle kidney cancer using generative AI and massive multimodal data.
item.content:encoded:
I am excited to announce that our lab has been awarded 2.9M$ from the Medical Research Council to tackle kidney cancer using generative AI and massive multimodal data.

Read the full announcement on X

item.title: Starting January 2026 I have joined the scientific advisory board of Novo Nordisk
item.link: https://lotfollahi.com/blog/Joined scientific advisory board of Novo Nordisk
item.guid: https://lotfollahi.com/blog/Joined scientific advisory board of Novo Nordisk
item.description: I am pleased to announce that starting January 2026, I have joined the scientific advisory board of Novo Nordisk.
item.content:encoded: I am pleased to announce that starting January 2026, I have joined the scientific advisory board of Novo Nordisk.

item.title: Appointed as director of ML/AI research at Relation Therapeutics
item.link: https://lotfollahi.com/blog/Appointed as director of ML/AI research at Relation Therapeutics
item.guid: https://lotfollahi.com/blog/Appointed as director of ML/AI research at Relation Therapeutics
item.description: I am thrilled to announce my new role as Director of ML Research at
item.content:encoded:
I am thrilled to announce my new role as Director of ML Research at Relation Therapeutics while keeping my academic appointments!

Exciting times are ahead for ML and drug discovery.

Read the press release on:

[Twitter], [Linkedin].

item.title: I have joined ELLIS
item.description: I am excited to join ELLIS - the European Laboratory for Learning and Intelligent Systems.
item.content:encoded:
I am excited to join ELLIS - the European Laboratory for Learning and Intelligent Systems. Very grateful to fellows who supported me.

About ELLIS:

ELLIS is a pan-European AI network of excellence which focuses on fundamental science, technical innovation and societal impact. Founded in 2018, ELLIS builds upon machine learning as the driver for modern AI and aims to secure Europe’s sovereignty in this competitive field by creating a multi-centric AI research laboratory. ELLIS wants to ensure that the highest level of AI research is performed in the open societies of Europe and follows a three-pillar strategy to achieve that.

item.title: CPA goes on the cover!
item.description: I am excited that our work from my time at Meta AI and collaboration with Helmholtz Munich on modeling single-cell perturbations (e.g., drugs, disease, CRISPR manipulations) is now featured on the cover of Molecular systems biology.
item.content:encoded:
I am excited that our work from my time at Meta AI and collaboration with Helmholtz Munich on modeling single-cell perturbations (e.g., drugs, disease, CRISPR manipulations) is now featured on the cover of Molecular systems biology. Thanks to all collaborators and my co-authors for making this happen.

About the paper:

Lotfollahi, M+., Klimovskaia Susmelj+, A., De Donno, C+., Hetzel, L., Ji, Y., Ibarra, I. L., ... & Theis, F. J.

[Molecular Systems Biology (2023)], [code], [Facebook AI blogpost], [state of AI report 2021], [featured cover].

item.title: Bayer Foundation Early Excellence in Science Award
item.description: I am greatly honored by Bayer Foundation
item.content:encoded:
I am greatly honored by Bayer Foundation by awarding me the "Early Excellence in Science Award" and recognizing my work on "developing machine learning algorithms to understand large-scale single-cell omics data in health and disease to ultimately facilitate the advancement of precision medicine and AI-assisted drug discovery." Big thanks to my family, previous and current collaborators, and mentors.

Watch the award video here (click in the image)​

[Award Ceremony, [Read about the award].

item.title: MDSI Best Paper of the Year Award
item.link: https://lotfollahi.com/blog/MDSI Best Paper of the Year Award
item.guid: https://lotfollahi.com/blog/MDSI Best Paper of the Year Award
item.description: Proud that our paper on life-long and transfer learning for single-cell biology has been awarded among top 3 papers selected by anonymous reviewers from MDSI at Technical University of Munich.
item.content:encoded:
Proud that our paper on life-long and transfer learning for single-cell biology has been awarded among top 3 papers selected by anonymous reviewers from MDSI at Technical University of Munich. This was among the work done during my doctoral studies at Helmholtz Munich and Life science school at TUM.

About the paper:

Mapping single-cell data to reference atlases by transfer learning

Lotfollahi, M., Naghipourfar, M., Luecken, M. D., Khajavi, M., Büttner, M., Wagenstetter, M., Avsec, Ž., Gayoso, A., Yosef, N., Interlandi, M. & Others.

[Nature Biotechnology (2022)], [code], [MDSI best paper award], [featured cover in Nature Biotechnology].
